reflection.quote: The most regretful people on earth are those who felt the call to creative work, who felt their own creative power restive and uprising, and gave to it neither power nor time.
reflection.reading:
Saturday in May. The kind of day that, years ago, would have already been written off by noon — hungover, foggy, half-promising yourself you'd do better next weekend. The hours blurred into each other. You couldn't tell Saturday from Sunday because both were spent recovering from Friday.

Now consider what a Saturday actually contains when you're awake for it. The light at 7am before anyone else is up. Coffee that you actually taste. A trail, a project, a friend who texts back. The errands that used to feel like a punishment because they cut into drinking time, and now feel like — what, exactly? Just life. Just the texture of being a person. Mary Oliver wasn't talking about sobriety, but she may as well have been: the regret isn't about the big swings you didn't take. It's about the hours you handed over without thinking. The Saturdays you traded for nothing.

You don't have to make today important. You don't have to summit anything. The point is just that you're here for it — that when the day ends, you'll remember it, and that the version of you who shows up Monday will have actually rested, actually moved, actually lived inside these hours. That's the quiet work. It looks like nothing from the outside. From the inside, it's everything.
reflection.meditation: What would it mean to be fully present for one ordinary hour today?

affirmations: I am awake for my own life today, and that is not a small thing.
Today I get to choose what fills these hours, and I trust myself to choose well.
I am building a life I don't need to escape from.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Saturday I'll actually remember. Grateful for the quiet morning, the body that works, the people who know my real name and my real story. Grateful that the hardest part — the part where I couldn't imagine any of this — is behind me, and that today I get to live inside what I once couldn't picture.
gratitude.closing: One more day, one more Saturday, lived awake.
